The figures below show a permanent magnet being moved into the middle of a coil/solenoid. The movement of the permanent magnet results in a changing magnetic field within the solenoid. This change in the magnetic field then has the effect of inducing a current into the solenoid (as shown by the deflection of the galvanometer).

Referring to the figures, which of the following statements is correct?
Question answer value 1 (for all questio: The current induced will increase if the magnet moved into the solenoid at a faster rate.
Question answer value 2: The current induced by the changing magnetic field will be reduced if the magnetic field changes at a faster rate.

This change in the magnetic field then has the effect of inducing a current into the solenoid (as shown by the deflection of the galvanometer).

Referring to the figures, which of the following statements is correct?
